business.headline: June retail inflation climbs to 4.38%, breaches RBI's 4% target after 5 monthsThe latest reading marks the first time since January 2026 that headline inflation has crossed the RBI's 4% target. Retail inflation had eased to 2.74% in January before gradually rising to 3.21% in February, 3.40% in March, 3.48% in April and 3.93% in May.

Piyush Goyal dismisses report claiming India is holding back on US trade dealThe rebuttal comes at a sensitive stage in India-US trade negotiations, with both sides racing to bridge differences on tariffs and market access ahead of a proposed bilateral trade agreement.

Why SK Hynix shares crashed over 15% days after their Nasdaq debutJust days after making a strong start in the US market, the South Korean chipmaker's shares plunged more than 15% on Monday, as investors rushed to book profits.

Delhi's property boom is creating an unusual courtroom problemWhat do soaring home prices have to do with courtrooms? According to the Delhi High Court, more than you might think. Here's the unusual problem now under scrutiny.

Why is crude oil back on the boil?Higher crude oil prices increase the country's import bill and can put pressure on inflation if fuel and transportation costs rise. They can also weaken the rupee as oil marketing companies require more US dollars to pay for imports.

Why TCS, Infosys and HCLTech shares rose despite a weak marketShares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech and LTIMindtree rose in early trade, outperforming the broader market and signalling renewed investor interest in a sector that has been under pressure for months.

What's behind India's tougher stance in trade talks with USThe US entered trade talks from a position of strength. But a series of developments may be tilting the balance towards New Delhi. Here's why India is in no hurry to strike a deal.

Sensex falls over 300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Indian benchmark indices fell sharply after renewed US-Iran hostilities lifted crude oil concerns. The sell-off reflected worries over India's import bill, inflation and the market impact if Brent climbs above $90.

Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo down 3%The BSE Sensex fell 626.40 points, or 0.81%, to 76,942.99, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 184 points, or 0.76%, to 24,022.90 as of 9:25 am.

A joint loan, a separation and a damaged credit score: Her story is a warningShe wasn't the one who defaulted, yet her loan application was affected. He never borrowed the money, yet he had to repay the debt. These two real-life stories reveal the hidden risks of saying 'yes' to someone else's loan. Could the same happen to you?

Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

entertainment.headline: Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi to release in 2027? Here's what we know Salman Khan's film Maatrubhumi faces a possible delay until 2027. The makers are hopeful for a Dussehra release if issues are resolved.

Made in India fame Lakshvir Saran says Naseeruddin Shah's praise is beyond awards In an exclusive conversation with India Today, actor Lakshvir Singh Saran, who was seen in Made in India: A Titan Story, reflects on building a real-life character with minimal references, the power of subtle romance, and why honest, inspiring storytelling matters in Indian streaming.

How a 6-year-old's cancer journey inspired The India Story's controversial subject The India Story director Chettan DK has shared that the film was inspired by the cancer diagnosis of a six-year-old child. His comments come as the Kajal Aggarwal-Shreyas Talpade starrer faces legal scrutiny ahead of its release.

Disgusting, man child: Dollyy Javved hits back at Kushal Tandon after Alliance exit After her eviction from The Alliance, Dollyy Javved launched a blistering attack on actor Kushal Tandon. Calling him egoistic, Javved claimed he tried manipulating everyone.

Producer Naga Vamsi says Lenin is Rs 61 crore strong, distributors in profit Producer Naga Vamsi celebrated Lenin's box-office run with a note on its theatrical success. He said the film's biggest achievement was helping distributors and exhibitors recover quickly.

Explained: How Anupam Kher's Ram Mandir theft remarks led to a controversy Anupam Kher said the reported theft at Ayodhya's Ram Mandir should be treated as a crime driven by greed, not as a stain on the temple or faith. His remarks drew backlash online after he compared the episode with far graver historical attacks on temples.

Don't Trouble the Trouble teaser: Fahadh Faasil meets genie in fantasy drama The first teaser for Don't Trouble the Trouble introduces Fahadh Faasil's magician Suri and a mysterious child. Their playful clash hints at a fantasy adventure powered by mischief, humour and wishes.

Why is Dhanush-Vetri Maaran's Thamizh Murugan under fire? 5 key questions answered The first look of Dhanush and Vetri Maaran's Thamizh Murugan has triggered a debate over how Murugan is portrayed. The row centres on Arivumathi's source book, which views Murugan through history and Tamil identity rather than divinity.

RGV calls Akhil best actor in Akkineni family, Chiranjeevi praises his act in Lenin As Lenin continues its strong theatrical run, praise for Akhil Akkineni has poured in from across the Telugu film industry. Chiranjeevi, Nagarjuna, Naga Chaitanya, Ram Gopal Varma and others highlighted both his performance and the film's emotional impact.

Kumar Sanu's son Jaan hospitalised after testing Covid-positive, cracks China joke Singer Kumar Sanu's son, Jaan Kumar Sanu, has been hospitalised after testing positive for COVID-19. The former Bigg Boss 14 contestant shared the update from the hospital and lightened the mood by cracking a "China se aaya mera dost" joke.

Inside Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and Sharan Sharma's star-studded wedding reception Actor Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and filmmaker Sharan Sharma hosted a wedding reception for family, friends, and film colleagues after their registered marriage on July 11. Actors Bobby Deol and Neena Gupta were among the attendees.

Ranveer Singh to start Pralay shoot in August, take paternity break around Diwali Actor Ranveer Singh will begin shooting for Jai Mehta's Pralay in August. Singh, who is set to welcome his second child with Deepika Padukone, is expected to take paternity leave by Diwali.

How Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj made Sidhu Moose Wala's song from 2022 trend again Late Sidhu Moose Wala's 2022 song Scapegoat is back in the news after the Diljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj was banned soon after OTT release. Read on to know why.

Anupam Kher says Dhurandhar is Rs 1,000-crore slap to those calling it a propaganda Actor Anupam Kher said he no longer reacts to claims that his films are propaganda. In an exclusive interview, he argued that audience response and box-office results matter more than such labels.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan uncut version off to a fabulous start, mints Rs 1 crore in UK Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's farewell film, Jana Nayagan, has crossed Rs 1 crore in UK advance bookings after its uncut release was confirmed for July 24. The response underlines strong overseas interest in Vijay's final film despite months of delay and controversy.

Football is life! Ted Lasso's Cristo Fernandez makes real-life football debut in US Ted Lasso actor Cristo Fernandez made his US professional football debut for El Paso Locomotive FC in a 2-0 home defeat to New Mexico United. The appearance echoed his role as Dani Rojas and marked his return to the professional game after injury.

Women are meant to nurture: Kangana backs Anandiben Patel's 'learn to cook' remark Kangana Ranaut backed Uttar Pradesh Governor Anandiben Patel's remarks urging women to learn to cook for their families. Her endorsement drew criticism online and widened the debate over gender roles.

Veteran music composer Pyarelal's wife Sunila Sharma dies in Mumbai at 78 Veteran music composer Pyarelal's wife, Sunila Sharma, died in Mumbai on July 12 at the age of 78. Her family announced the news in a social media statement, saying she died peacefully surrounded by her family.

Meet Imtiaz Ali's daughter Ida, whose dreamy proposal won the internet Imtiaz Ali's daughter Ida Ali is engaged to longtime boyfriend Krish Agrawal after a dreamy proposal in Norway. Here's everything to know about the filmmaker, her work and the viral engagement.

general news.headline: History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sHarmanpreet Kaur led India to a 270-run win over England as India won the first-ever women's Test at Lord's. Yastika Bhatia's second-innings hundred and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul in the first were the highlights of the historic win.

UK declares Iran's IRGC, another Iran-linked group terror organisations over antisemitic attacks in BritainThe UK Home Office said ministers had concluded there was sufficient evidence to believe the IRGC had engaged in activities that threatened the UK's national security.

The next oil shock won't shake India like in the past. Story of a 90-day shiftThe Iran war delivered a jolt to India's energy security, exposing the risks of heavy reliance on the Middle East. Even as the conflict gets reignited after US strikes, India's state-run refiners seem to be better-positioned to deal with geopolitical disruptions. Here's how a shift took place in 90 days.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup winAustralia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

Why tapped solar electricity is going down the drain in IndiaWhile solar power generation in India has increased manifold, storage capacity lags drastically. That's why electricity generated from the sun is going down the drain. The challenge is to make solar power available when consumers need it the most.

Mother and son graduate together from IIT Madras at 45 and 21At the IIT Madras convocation, Jigisha Tailor and her son Aditya Kapadia received degrees from the same online data science programme together. The unexpected moment capped a journey of shared study, family support and friendly competition.

El Nino shows true colours, makes India's monsoon gasp for rain cloudsIndia's monsoon has entered a fresh break phase as El Nino suppresses rain-bearing systems. Forecasters expect below-normal rainfall for now, with a possible revival later in July.

How an Indian startup is using software to break China's rare earth monopolyHandcrafted ByOur Editors A Bengaluru-based startup is building motors without permanent magnets. By doing so, it is trying to break China's stronghold on rare earth minerals. India Today Tech reports.

Thousands of dog-owners had to put pets to death in Punjab; a terrorism horror storyDiljit Dosanjh's Satluj has brought the dangerous days of terrorism in Punjab into the spotlight. One well-documented aspect of those days is the massacre of thousands of dogs across the state. At a time when we are debating how best or kindly to deal with street dogs, it is instructive to read this horror story about why Punjab's dogs became collateral damage.

Odisha on alert after two COVID-19 deaths reported in neighbouring Andhra PradeshFollowing reports of fresh COVID-19 cases and deaths in neighbouring Andhra Pradesh, Odisha has stepped up surveillance and testing as a precaution. Health officials say no positive cases have been detected in the state so far.

Live: US strikes kill 2 in Iran; Houthis detain Red Cross plane in YemenUS-Iran War Live Updates: Fighting between the United States and Iran has intensified sharply, with both sides exchanging fresh missile and drone strikes on Monday. Iran says it has once again closed the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, while the US insists the international waterway remains open to commercial shipping. The latest attacks have reached Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Jordan, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain, raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Washington has carried out fresh strikes on Iranian military targets around Hormuz, while Tehran has targeted US-linked military facilities across the Gulf and vowed to defend the key shipping route. The renewed violence has cast serious doubt on last month's interim US-Iran agreement, which was intended to reopen the strait and pave the way for a broader peace deal after 60 days of negotiations. President Donald Trump has declared the ceasefire effectively over, even as he says he remains open to talks. The escalating conflict has also disrupted one of the world's busiest energy corridors, pushing up oil prices and raising concerns over global trade. Iran has warned ships not to enter the strait without its approval, while the US says its forces are positioned to protect freedom of navigation. As military operations continue, Gulf nations are dealing with missile and drone attacks, civilian injuries and damage to critical infrastructure, while diplomatic efforts led by Oman, Qatar and other regional partners struggle to keep pace. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest developments, key statements and real-time analysis from the rapidly evolving US-Iran conflict.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken?India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Puja held inside moving train, Railways clarifies after video goes viralA video showing a priest conducting a puja inside a moving train coach went viral online. Northern Railways said the ritual took place in a privately booked saloon car, prompting debate over activities allowed during journeys.

Women are meant to nurture: Kangana backs Anandiben Patel's 'learn to cook' remarkKangana Ranaut backed Uttar Pradesh Governor Anandiben Patel's remarks urging women to learn to cook for their families. Her endorsement drew criticism online and widened the debate over gender roles.

Is Physics the toughest Olympiad? India's five-gold triumph makes the caseIndia's clean sweep at the 56th International Physics Olympiad is more than a historic achievement. It makes a compelling case for the Physics Olympiad as one of the world's toughest academic competitions, where success demands not just mastery of theory but exceptional experimental skill and scientific thinking.

Sonam Wangchuk loses 8.2 kg in 16 days of hunger strike at Cockroach Party protestSonam Wangchuk's health declined further on the 16th day of his indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. As opposition leaders and student groups rallied behind the protest, organisers urged the Centre to act on their demands.

'I almost died eight times': Women blame Zara's viral trousers for nasty fallsWomen on TikTok and Instagram have blamed Zara's viral wide-leg trousers for a string of falls and injuries. The posts have fuelled jokes online and renewed questions about the practical risks of ultra-wide silhouettes.

Watch: Meant for a helicopter, fired from the ground. Russian gun goes rogueThe viral footage shows the Russian soldier preparing to fire the four-barrel YakB-12.7, which had been bolted onto a ground mount instead of its intended platform, the nose turret of a Soviet-era Mi-24 attack helicopter.

Man quits corporate career after 18 years, says office politics cost him promotionDharmendra Pandey, who describes himself in his Instagram bio as someone "helping working professionals build an income outside their job," posted a video reflecting on his nearly two decades in the corporate world and the lessons he learnt before quitting.

Naseeruddin Shah called Anupam Kher a clown. Video resurfaces amid Ram Temple rowAn old video of Naseeruddin Shah calling Anupam Kher a clown has resurfaced after Kher's remarks on the theft of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple.

Video: Cop allows 6-year-old granddaughter to drive car, gets confronted by localsA video of the incident showed the 6-year-old slowly driving the Maruti Swift on a busy road while the police officer sat in the front passenger seat with one hand on the steering wheel.

Piyush Goyal dismisses report claiming India is holding back on US trade dealThe rebuttal comes at a sensitive stage in India-US trade negotiations, with both sides racing to bridge differences on tariffs and market access ahead of a proposed bilateral trade agreement.

How a war turned Argentina vs England football into India-Pak-like showdownA war over a remote South Atlantic archipelago turned Argentina vs England into football's closest equivalent of the India-Pakistan cricket rivalry. From Maradona's brilliance to modern-day controversies, every meeting between the Three Lions and the Albiceleste carries the weight of history, national pride and football.

Meet 12-year-old Mana Jampala who built an AI startup with clients in 3 countriesMana Jampala is only 12, but she is already running an AI startup with deployments in Canada, India and Cambodia. After learning Python at nine and building AI products by 11, the Grade 7 student has developed AI-powered tools that help businesses automate customer calls and everyday operations.

sports.headline: Indian cricket team hammers England to win first-ever women's Test at Lord's Harmanpreet Kaur led India to a 270-run win over England as India won the first-ever women's Test at Lord's. Yastika Bhatia's second-innings hundred and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul in the first were the highlights of the historic win.

Sachin drops in at Lord's, leads pep talk as India women chase historic win Sachin Tendulkar paid a surprise visit to the Indian women's team at Lord's ahead of the fourth day of the historic one-off Test against England. He was seen leading the talk to the team as Harmanpreet Kaur's side closed in on a famous victory.

Day 4 live: India win by 270 runs, win first-ever women's Test at Lord's England (ENG) vs India (IND) Day 4 live score, women's one-off Test: Harmanpreet Kaur's India win the first ever women's Test match at Lord's. India beat England by a massive margin of 270 runs.

After dashing Wimbledon appearance, Sanju Samson reveals Novak Djokovic inspiration At Wimbledon, Sanju Samson said Novak Djokovic's discipline and demeanour remain a benchmark for athletes. Yuvraj Singh backed that view, highlighting Djokovic's self-belief, longevity and influence on younger players across the globe.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup win Australia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

How Sachin Tendulkar is backing a quiet sporting revolution in Bastar Sachin Tendulkar visited Dantewada to support Bastar's Maidan Cup and meet village children. The initiative is expanding community playgrounds and restoring confidence through sport in a region shaped by insurgency.

Shreyas Iyer's interview over Sooryavanshi snub irks ex-captain: Not the way to talk Kris Srikkanth criticised Shreyas Iyer and the team management for dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Southampton T20I. He said the move and Iyer's remarks showed poor handling of a young player.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helm IPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken? India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Andy Flower took England to No.1: Nasser backs RCB coach to replace McCullum Former England captain Nasser Hussain has backed Andy Flower to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test head coach, saying his meticulous approach is exactly what the team needs after the Bazball era.

Zverev aims to move beyond tennis' third guy tag, close gap on Sinner and Alcaraz Wimbledon runner-up Alexander Zverev will return to world No. 2 on Monday, but believes his attacking game has finally brought him closer to Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz after years of being labelled tennis' "third guy".

Not at full throttle, Jannik Sinner still stands atop the tennis worldJannik Sinner proved that true champions are not defined only by dominance but by their ability to survive adversity and find a way to win. On Sunday, the Italian showcased exactly that, defending his Wimbledon title with a hard-fought victory over Alexander Zverev in the final.

Yastika Bhatia reveals how Mandhana's advice fuelled her Lord's comeback centuryINDW vs ENGW, one-off Test: Yastika Bhatia credited Smriti Mandhana's advice for helping her overcome a serious knee injury after marking her comeback with a maiden international century at Lord's.

The Bazball verdict: Brilliant at home, exposed by the eliteBrendon McCullum's Bazball promised to revolutionise England's Test cricket. Four years later, sacked after a humiliating Ashes defeat, the numbers reveal the truth: it worked at home and against weak teams. Against India and Australia, it crumbled.

Lord's Test: Yastika Bhatia, Kranti Gaud leave India on verge of famous win at Day 3Yastika Bhatia became the first woman to score a Test hundred at Lord's before Kranti Gaud's fiery spell left England six down as India took complete control on Day 3 of the historic Women's Test, setting the hosts a daunting 457-run target.

Alexander Zverev's runner-up finish fuels belief he can one day win WimbledonAlexander Zverev said reaching his maiden Wimbledon final has convinced him he can one day lift the title despite losing to Jannik Sinner. The German praised the world No. 1 while reflecting on a breakthrough fortnight at the All England Club.

Ice-cool Jannik Sinner fights off feisty Alexander Zverev to retain crownWorld No.1 Jannik Sinner came from a set down to beat Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday, July 12 on Centre Court.

Rahul Dravid among candidates to replace Brendon McCullum as England Test coachRahul Dravid has reportedly emerged as a contender to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. The former India coach features on a shortlist that includes Andy Flower and Richard Dawson, though the report says he is reluctant to coach full-time.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes Wimbledon debut, picks favourite tennis starTeen cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enjoyed his first Wimbledon visit, attending the men's singles final alongside Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson. The 15-year-old also revealed his admiration for Novak Djokovic and backed Jannik Sinner.

Wimbledon Highlights: Sinner crowned champion again, beat Zverev in finalWimbledon Final Highlights: Jannik Sinner has successfully defended his Wimbledon crown after beating Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 in three hours and 46 minutes on Centre Court.

End of Bazball as McCullum sacked as Test coach after Ben Stokes retirementBrendon McCullum has been sacked as England's Test head coach, just days after Ben Stokes announced his retirement from international cricket. However, the former New Zealand captain will continue in his role as head coach of England's ODI and T20I sides.

Yastika Bhatia becomes first woman to hit Test century at Lord'sEngland vs India, only Test: Yastika Bhatia became the first batter to score a century in a women's Test at Lord's Cricket Ground as India dominated proceedings on Day 3 of the iconic match.

FIFA will explore expanding World Cup to 64 teams before 2030 edition: InfantinoFIFA president confirmed discussions for a 64-team tournament will take place ahead of the 2030 centenary edition, insisting football's global showpiece must look beyond the traditional dominance of Europe and South America.

India tour of England, Zimbabwe: Prince and Bishnoi named injury replacementsIndia have brought in Prince Yadav and Ravi Bishnoi after Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy suffered hamstring injuries during the recently-concluded England T20Is. The reshuffle comes ahead of the ODIs in England and the T20Is in Zimbabwe.

Is cricket's DRS better than football's VAR?Football and cricket embraced video reviews to eliminate costly mistakes. But one sport gave players the power to challenge decisions, while the other left it to officials, shaping every debate around VAR and DRS since.

Lionel Scaloni dismisses Argentina-England history before World Cup semi-finalFIFA World Cup: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni played down his country's history with England, insisting that the semi-final was "just a football game" after his side defeated Switzerland 3-1 in the quarter-finals.

IPL puts heavy make-up on batters: Sanjay Manjrekar on India's T20I debacleFormer India batter Sanjay Manjrekar believes India's disappointing T20I tour should shift the focus from the players to the IPL, saying the tournament's batting-friendly conditions do not adequately prepare batters for the demands of overseas cricket.

Preach patience, practice panic: How India failed Sooryavanshi and Samson in the UKIndia's 0-6 tour of the United Kingdom will be remembered for the records it shattered, but perhaps its biggest failure lay elsewhere. In the space of one disastrous series, India risked damaging the confidence of two batters at opposite ends of their careers, raising uncomfortable questions about the team's handling of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sanju Samson.

Argentina bias at FIFA World Cup? Swiss coach slams unacceptable Embolo red cardSwitzerland coach Murat Yakin blasted the rule behind Breel Embolo's controversial red card as "unacceptable" after Argentina scored twice in extra time to seal a 3-1 win and end the Swiss fairytale in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Saturday.

Messi's heated exchange with Portuguese referee goes viral: Don't be disrespectfulLionel Messi found himself at the center of another viral World Cup moment, this time after a heated exchange with Portuguese referee João Pinheiro during Argentina's dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over Switzerland.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
